title: leucothoe racemosa var. elongata
intro: Leucothoe racemosa var. elongata, also known as the eastern doghobble, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, native to North America. Its medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antiseptic. Leucothoe racemosa var. elongata is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
